questions.question: Explain the different types of system calls with examples for each category.
keyPoints: Process Control: fork(), exec(), exit(), wait(), abort() - manage process lifecycle
File Management: open(), close(), read(), write(), lseek() - manage files
Device Management: open(), close(), read(), write(), ioctl() - manage I/O devices
Information Maintenance: getpid(), time(), sleep() - get/set system information
Communication: pipe(), shmget(), socket(), send(), recv() - inter-process communication
Protection: chmod(), chown(), umask() - access control and security

questions.question: Explain the fork() and exec() system calls with an example showing how they work together.
keyPoints: fork() creates a child process by duplicating the parent
Returns child PID to parent, returns 0 to child
exec() replaces current process image with a new program
Typically child calls exec() after fork() to run different program
Parent can call wait() to wait for child completion
Provide code example showing fork-exec pattern

questions.question: Compare message passing and shared memory models of inter-process communication.
keyPoints: Message passing uses kernel as intermediary (pipe, socket)
Shared memory allows direct memory access between processes
Message passing is slower but easier with built-in synchronization
Shared memory is faster but requires explicit synchronization
Message passing works across network, shared memory is local only
